revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Three stages of Japan]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

'''日本三舞台'''（にほんさんぶたい）は、[[四天王寺]]（[[大阪市]][[天王寺区]]）の石舞台、[[住吉大社]]（大阪市[[住吉区]]）の石舞台、[[厳島神社]]（[[広島県]][[廿日市市]]）の板舞台。
いずれの舞台においても古式ゆかしい雅な[[雅楽]]が演じられることでも知られる。
[[Category:名数|3 にほんさんふたい]]

'''日本三舞台'''（にほんさんぶたい）は、[[四天王寺]]（[[大阪市]][[天王寺区]]）の石[[舞台]]、[[住吉大社]]（大阪市[[住吉区]]）の石舞台、[[厳島神社]]（[[広島県]][[廿日市市]]）の板舞台。
いずれの舞台においても古式ゆかしい雅な[[雅楽]]が演じられることでも知られる。
{{DEFAULTSORT:にほんさんふたい}} [[Category:日本の劇場]] [[Category:名数3|ふたい]]

'''日本三舞台'''（にほんさんぶたい）は、[[四天王寺]]（[[大阪市]][[天王寺区]]）の石[[舞台]]、[[住吉大社]]（大阪市[[住吉区]]）の石舞台、[[厳島神社]]（[[広島県]][[廿日市市]]）の板舞台。
いずれの舞台においても古式ゆかしい雅な[[雅楽]]が演じられることでも知られる。
{{DEFAULTSORT:にほんさんふたい}} [[Category:日本の劇場|*]] [[Category:名数3|ふたい]]

'''日本三舞台'''（にほんさんぶたい）は、[[舞楽]]を奉納するための舞台が備えられている、[[四天王寺]]（[[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[天王寺区]]）の石舞台、[[住吉大社]]（大阪府大阪市[[住吉区]]）の石舞台、[[厳島神社]]（別名: 平舞台、高舞台）（[[広島県]][[廿日市市]]）の板舞台からなる3つの歴史ある社寺に備えられた舞台のことをいう<ref>{{Cite web|title=日本三舞台とは!?|url=https://tabi-mag.jp/stage-big3/|website=ニッポン旅マガジン|date=2020-02-04|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=【旅探（たびたん）】厳島神社｜日本の世界遺産|url=https://www.homemate-research-tour.com/useful/jp_world_heritage/heritage/itsukushima.php|website=www.homemate-research-tour.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=MORNING ALIVE BLOG|url=http://hfm.jp/blog/morning/2008/01/post_205.html|website=hfm.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=数で知る宮島｜一般社団法人宮島観光協会|url=https://www.miyajima.or.jp/pickup/number.html|website=www.miyajima.or.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref>。
== 一覧 ==
* [[四天王寺]]（[[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[天王寺区]]）の石舞台 - 亀の池のところに位置し、国の重要指定文化遺産に登録されている<ref>{{Cite web|title=石舞台と豊臣家 {{!}} コラム|url=https://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/|website=住吉大社|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=四天王寺の石舞台。亀の池の所にあります。 住吉大社の石舞台、厳島神社の平舞台と並んで日本三舞台だそうです。 - Shitennoji, Osaka Resmi - Tripadvisor|url=https://www.tripadvisor.com.tr/LocationPhotoDirectLink-g298566-d321316-i379394922-Shitennoji-Osaka_Osaka_Prefecture_Kinki.html|website=www.tripadvisor.com.tr|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=tr}}</ref>。 * [[住吉大社]]（大阪府大阪市[[住吉区]]）の石舞台 - 慶長年間に豊臣秀頼によって奉納された地で、日本の重要文化財に指定されている<ref>{{Cite web|title=大阪景観情報サイトおおさか景観.com｜大阪ええ眺めセレクション|url=https://osaka-keikan.com/view.php?pid=39|website=osaka-keikan.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref> * [[厳島神社]]（別名: 平舞台、高舞台）（[[広島県]][[廿日市市]]）の板舞台 - 能の舞台としても使用されており、国宝に登録されている<ref>{{Cite web|title=厳島神社｜日本三舞台｜日本各地の情報をお届け|url=https://nippon-now.com/spots/detail/spot34.html|website=日本の魅力を再発見！NIPPON NOW|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=【日本三景】国内外問わず人気。海の上にたたずむ安芸の宮島「厳島神社」 {{!}} RETRIP[リトリップ]|url=https://rtrp.jp/articles/981/|website=RETRIP[リトリップ] - 旅行・おでかけメディア|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}{{Theat-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:にほんさんふたい}} [[Category:日本の劇場|*]] [[Category:日本の名数3|ふたい]]

revision.comment: Created page with "[[File:Sumiyoshi jinja Otaue.jpg|thumb|360px|Sumiyoshi Taisha Otaue Ritual.]] '''Sumiyoshi no Otaue''' (住吉の御田植) is a traditional folk performing art and rice-planting ritual (田楽) associated with the {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田植祭}} at {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}} in {{ill|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|ja|住吉区}}, Osaka, Japan. Since the beginning of {{ill|rice cultivation|ja|稲作}} in Japanese history, rituals to honor the {{ill|Ta-no-Kami|j..."

revision.text:
[[File:Sumiyoshi jinja Otaue.jpg|thumb|360px|Sumiyoshi Taisha Otaue Ritual.]] t the {{ill|Stone Stage|en}}[[ja:日本三舞台]] and attend a dedication ceremony at the First Shrine (第一本宮). The procession, including musicians, {{ill|yaotome|en}}[[ja:八乙女]], ceremonial women, planters, substitutes, children attendants, warriors in colorful garb, and dancers of the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Odori|en}}[[ja:住吉踊り]], then heads to the rice field.
After sacred water is sprinkled around the field to purify it, rice seedlings are handed over for planting. Planting begins when seedlings are transferred from the planters to substitutes. Throughout the ritual, various performances are offered, including rice-planting dances by the yaotome, mock battles between red and white teams of warriors using bells, drums, and {{ill|conch shells|en}}[[ja:ほら貝]], and performances of Sumiyoshi Odori, in which children dressed as monks dance around a large umbrella held by a leader chanting songs. The dances and rituals aim to honor and enhance the spiritual power of the rice.
On {{ill|February 3, 1979|en}}[[ja:1979年2月3日]], Sumiyoshi no Otaue was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|en}}[[ja:重要無形民俗文化財]].
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Theatre of Japan|lt=Traditional performing arts of Japan|en}}[[ja:伝統芸能|en]]
== External Links == * [https://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/events/special.html#anchor01 Otaue (Official Sumiyoshi Taisha website)] Accessed September 22, 2009. * {{Cultural Heritage Online|302|103|住吉の御田植]]

[[File:Sumiyoshi jinja Otaue.jpg|thumb|360px|Sumiyoshi Taisha Otaue Ritual.]]
'''Nihon San Butai''' (日本三舞台) refers to three historic stages built for the performance of [[Bugaku]] in Japan. These stages are located at the following significant temple and shrine sites:
* The **Stone Stage of Shitenno-ji Temple** in [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. * The **Stone Stage of Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine** in Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture. * The **Board Stage of Itsukushima Shrine** (also known as **Flat Stage** or **High Stage**) in [[Hatsukaichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nihon San Butai Explained|url=https://tabi-mag.jp/stage-big3/|website=Japan Travel Magazine|date=2020-02-04|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Japan's World Heritage Sites|url=https://www.homemate-research-tour.com/useful/jp_world_heritage/heritage/itsukushima.php|website=www.homemate-research-tour.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=MORNING ALIVE BLOG|url=http://hfm.jp/blog/morning/2008/01/post_205.html|website=hfm.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Miyajima by the Numbers - Miyajima Tourism Association|url=https://www.miyajima.or.jp/pickup/number.html|website=www.miyajima.or.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref>
== List of Stages ==
* **Shitenno-ji Temple** (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage is located at the "Turtle Pond" and is registered as a nationally designated cultural heritage site.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Stone Stage and the Toyotomi Family|url=https://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/|website=Sumiyoshi Taisha|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Shitenno-ji's Stone Stage at Turtle Pond - One of Nihon San Butai|url=https://www.tripadvisor.com.tr/LocationPhotoDirectLink-g298566-d321316-i379394922-Shitennoji-Osaka_Osaka_Prefecture_Kinki.html|website=Tripadvisor|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=tr}}</ref> * **Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine** (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage was donated by **Toyotomi Hideyori** during the Keicho era and is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Osaka Scenic Information Site|url=https://osaka-keikan.com/view.php?pid=39|website=osaka-keikan.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref> * **Itsukushima Shrine** (Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture) - Also known as the **Flat Stage** or **High Stage**, this wooden board stage is used for **Noh performances** and is designated as a **National Treasure of Japan**.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Nihon San Butai|url=https://nippon-now.com/spots/detail/spot34.html|website=NIPPON NOW|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Japan's Three Scenic Views - The Floating Shrine of Itsukushima|url=https://rtrp.jp/articles/981/|website=RETRIP - Travel Media|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:Nihon San Butai}} [[Category:Theaters in Japan]] [[Category:Sets of three in Japan]]
t the {{ill|Stone Stage|en}}[[ja:日本三舞台]] and attend a dedication ceremony at the First Shrine (第一本宮). The procession, including musicians, {{ill|yaotome|en}}[[ja:八乙女]], ceremonial women, planters, substitutes, children attendants, warriors in colorful garb, and dancers of the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Odori|en}}[[ja:住吉踊り]], then heads to the rice field.
After sacred water is sprinkled around the field to purify it, rice seedlings are handed over for planting. Planting begins when seedlings are transferred from the planters to substitutes. Throughout the ritual, various performances are offered, including rice-planting dances by the yaotome, mock battles between red and white teams of warriors using bells, drums, and {{ill|conch shells|en}}[[ja:ほら貝]], and performances of Sumiyoshi Odori, in which children dressed as monks dance around a large umbrella held by a leader chanting songs. The dances and rituals aim to honor and enhance the spiritual power of the rice.
On {{ill|February 3, 1979|en}}[[ja:1979年2月3日]], Sumiyoshi no Otaue was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|en}}[[ja:重要無形民俗文化財]].

'''Nihon San Butai''' (日本三舞台) refers to three historic stages built for the performance of [[Bugaku]] in Japan. These stages are located at the following significant temple and shrine sites:
* The **Stone Stage of Shitenno-ji Temple** in [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. * The **Stone Stage of Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine** in Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture. * The **Board Stage of Itsukushima Shrine** (also known as **Flat Stage** or **High Stage**) in [[Hatsukaichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nihon San Butai Explained|url=https://tabi-mag.jp/stage-big3/|website=Japan Travel Magazine|date=2020-02-04|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Japan's World Heritage Sites|url=https://www.homemate-research-tour.com/useful/jp_world_heritage/heritage/itsukushima.php|website=www.homemate-research-tour.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=MORNING ALIVE BLOG|url=http://hfm.jp/blog/morning/2008/01/post_205.html|website=hfm.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Miyajima by the Numbers - Miyajima Tourism Association|url=https://www.miyajima.or.jp/pickup/number.html|website=www.miyajima.or.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref>
== List of Stages ==
* **Shitenno-ji Temple** (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage is located at the "Turtle Pond" and is registered as a nationally designated cultural heritage site.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Stone Stage and the Toyotomi Family|url=https://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/|website=Sumiyoshi Taisha|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Shitenno-ji's Stone Stage at Turtle Pond - One of Nihon San Butai|url=https://www.tripadvisor.com.tr/LocationPhotoDirectLink-g298566-d321316-i379394922-Shitennoji-Osaka_Osaka_Prefecture_Kinki.html|website=Tripadvisor|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=tr}}</ref> * **Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine** (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage was donated by **Toyotomi Hideyori** during the Keicho era and is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Osaka Scenic Information Site|url=https://osaka-keikan.com/view.php?pid=39|website=osaka-keikan.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref> * **Itsukushima Shrine** (Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture) - Also known as the **Flat Stage** or **High Stage**, this wooden board stage is used for **Noh performances** and is designated as a **National Treasure of Japan**.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Nihon San Butai|url=https://nippon-now.com/spots/detail/spot34.html|website=NIPPON NOW|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Japan's Three Scenic Views - The Floating Shrine of Itsukushima|url=https://rtrp.jp/articles/981/|website=RETRIP - Travel Media|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref>

After sacred water is sprinkled around the field to purify it, rice seedlings are handed over for planting. Planting begins when seedlings are transferred from the planters to substitutes. [[File:Sumiyoshi jinja Otaue.jpg|thumb|360px|Sumiyoshi Taisha Otaue Ritual.]]
'''Nihon San Butai''' (日本三舞台) refers to three historic stages built for the performance of [[Bugaku]] in Japan. These stages are located at the following significant temple and shrine sites:
* The '''Stone Stage of Shitenno-ji Temple''' in [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. * The '''Stone Stage of Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' in Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture. * The '''Board Stage of Itsukushima Shrine''' (also known as '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''') in [[Hatsukaichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nihon San Butai Explained|url=https://tabi-mag.jp/stage-big3/|website=Japan Travel Magazine|date=2020-02-04|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Japan's World Heritage Sites|url=https://www.homemate-research-tour.com/useful/jp_world_heritage/heritage/itsukushima.php|website=www.homemate-research-tour.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=MORNING ALIVE BLOG|url=http://hfm.jp/blog/morning/2008/01/post_205.html|website=hfm.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Miyajima by the Numbers - Miyajima Tourism Association|url=https://www.miyajima.or.jp/pickup/number.html|website=www.miyajima.or.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref>
== List of Stages ==
* '''Shitenno-ji Temple''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage is located at the "Turtle Pond" and is registered as a nationally designated cultural heritage site.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Stone Stage and the Toyotomi Family|url=https://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/|website=Sumiyoshi Taisha|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Shitenno-ji's Stone Stage at Turtle Pond - One of Nihon San Butai|url=https://www.tripadvisor.com.tr/LocationPhotoDirectLink-g298566-d321316-i379394922-Shitennoji-Osaka_Osaka_Prefecture_Kinki.html|website=Tripadvisor|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=tr}}</ref> * '''Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage was donated by '''Toyotomi Hideyori''' during the Keicho era and is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Osaka Scenic Information Site|url=https://osaka-keikan.com/view.php?pid=39|website=osaka-keikan.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref> * '''Itsukushima Shrine''' (Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture) - Also known as the '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''', this wooden board stage is used for '''Noh performances''' and is designated as a '''National Treasure of Japan'''.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Nihon San Butai|url=https://nippon-now.com/spots/detail/spot34.html|website=NIPPON NOW|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Japan's Three Scenic Views - The Floating Shrine of Itsukushima|url=https://rtrp.jp/articles/981/|website=RETRIP - Travel Media|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:Nihon San Butai}} [[Category:Theaters in Japan]] [[Category:Sets of three in Japan]]
t the {{ill|Stone Stage|en}}[[ja:日本三舞台]] and attend a dedication ceremony at the First Shrine (第一本宮). The procession, including musicians, {{ill|yaotome|en}}[[ja:八乙女]], ceremonial women, planters, substitutes, children attendants, warriors in colorful garb, and dancers of the {{ill|Sumiyoshi Odori|en}}[[ja:住吉踊り]], then heads to the rice field.
After sacred water is sprinkled around the field to purify it, rice seedlings are handed over for planting. Planting begins when seedlings are transferred from the planters to substitutes. Throughout the ritual, various performances are offered, including rice-planting dances by the yaotome, mock battles between red and white teams of warriors using bells, drums, and {{ill|conch shells|en}}[[ja:ほら貝]], and performances of Sumiyoshi Odori, in which children dressed as monks dance around a large umbrella held by a leader chanting songs. The dances and rituals aim to honor and enhance the spiritual power of the rice.
On {{ill|February 3, 1979|en}}[[ja:1979年2月3日]], Sumiyoshi no Otaue was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|en}}[[ja:重要無形民俗文化財]].

[[File:Sumiyoshi jinja Otaue.jpg|thumb|360px|Sumiyoshi Taisha Otaue Ritual.]]
'''Nihon San Butai''' (日本三舞台) refers to three historic stages built for the performance of [[Bugaku]] in Japan. These stages are located at the following significant temple and shrine sites:
* The '''Stone Stage of Shitenno-ji Temple''' in [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. * The '''Stone Stage of Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' in Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture. * The '''Board Stage of Itsukushima Shrine''' (also known as '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''') in [[Hatsukaichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nihon San Butai Explained|url=https://tabi-mag.jp/stage-big3/|website=Japan Travel Magazine|date=2020-02-04|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Japan's World Heritage Sites|url=https://www.homemate-research-tour.com/useful/jp_world_heritage/heritage/itsukushima.php|website=www.homemate-research-tour.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=MORNING ALIVE BLOG|url=http://hfm.jp/blog/morning/2008/01/post_205.html|website=hfm.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Miyajima by the Numbers - Miyajima Tourism Association|url=https://www.miyajima.or.jp/pickup/number.html|website=www.miyajima.or.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref>
== List of Stages ==
* '''Shitenno-ji Temple''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage is located at the "Turtle Pond" and is registered as a nationally designated cultural heritage site.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Stone Stage and the Toyotomi Family|url=https://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/|website=Sumiyoshi Taisha|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Shitenno-ji's Stone Stage at Turtle Pond - One of Nihon San Butai|url=https://www.tripadvisor.com.tr/LocationPhotoDirectLink-g298566-d321316-i379394922-Shitennoji-Osaka_Osaka_Prefecture_Kinki.html|website=Tripadvisor|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=tr}}</ref> * '''Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage was donated by '''Toyotomi Hideyori''' during the Keicho era and is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Osaka Scenic Information Site|url=https://osaka-keikan.com/view.php?pid=39|website=osaka-keikan.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref> * '''Itsukushima Shrine''' (Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture) - Also known as the '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''', this wooden board stage is used for '''Noh performances''' and is designated as a '''National Treasure of Japan'''.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Nihon San Butai|url=https://nippon-now.com/spots/detail/spot34.html|website=NIPPON NOW|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Japan's Three Scenic Views - The Floating Shrine of Itsukushima|url=https://rtrp.jp/articles/981/|website=RETRIP - Travel Media|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref>

revision.text:
[[File:Sumiyoshi jinja Otaue.jpg|thumb|360px|Sumiyoshi Taisha Otaue Ritual.]]
'''Nihon San Butai''' (日本三舞台) refers to three historic stages built for the performance of [[Bugaku]] in Japan. These stages are located at the following significant temple and shrine sites:
* The '''Stone Stage of Shitenno-ji Temple''' in [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. * The '''Stone Stage of Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' in Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture. * The '''Board Stage of Itsukushima Shrine''' (also known as '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''') in [[Hatsukaichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nihon San Butai Explained|url=https://tabi-mag.jp/stage-big3/|website=Japan Travel Magazine|date=2020-02-04|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Japan's World Heritage Sites|url=https://www.homemate-research-tour.com/useful/jp_world_heritage/heritage/itsukushima.php|website=www.homemate-research-tour.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=MORNING ALIVE BLOG|url=http://hfm.jp/blog/morning/2008/01/post_205.html|website=hfm.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Miyajima by the Numbers - Miyajima Tourism Association|url=https://www.miyajima.or.jp/pickup/number.html|website=www.miyajima.or.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref>
== List of Stages ==
* '''Shitenno-ji Temple''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage is located at the "Turtle Pond" and is registered as a nationally designated cultural heritage site.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Stone Stage and the Toyotomi Family|url=https://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/|website=Sumiyoshi Taisha|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Shitenno-ji's Stone Stage at Turtle Pond - One of Nihon San Butai|url=https://www.tripadvisor.com.tr/LocationPhotoDirectLink-g298566-d321316-i379394922-Shitennoji-Osaka_Osaka_Prefecture_Kinki.html|website=Tripadvisor|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=tr}}</ref> * '''Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage was donated by '''Toyotomi Hideyori''' during the Keicho era and is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Osaka Scenic Information Site|url=https://osaka-keikan.com/view.php?pid=39|website=osaka-keikan.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref> * '''Itsukushima Shrine''' (Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture) - Also known as the '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''', this wooden board stage is used for '''Noh performances''' and is designated as a '''National Treasure of Japan'''.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Nihon San Butai|url=https://nippon-now.com/spots/detail/spot34.html|website=NIPPON NOW|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Japan's Three Scenic Views - The Floating Shrine of Itsukushima|url=https://rtrp.jp/articles/981/|website=RETRIP - Travel Media|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref>

[[File:Sumiyoshi jinja Otaue.jpg|thumb|360px|Sumiyoshi Taisha Otaue Ritual.]]
'''Nihon San Butai''' (日本三舞台) refers to three historic stages built for the performance of [[Bugaku]] in Japan. These stages are located at the following significant temple and shrine sites:
* The '''Stone Stage of Shitenno-ji Temple''' in [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. * The '''Stone Stage of Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' in Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture. * The '''Board Stage of Itsukushima Shrine''' (also known as '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''') in [[Hatsukaichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nihon San Butai Explained|url=https://tabi-mag.jp/stage-big3/|website=Japan Travel Magazine|date=2020-02-04|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Japan's World Heritage Sites|url=https://www.homemate-research-tour.com/useful/jp_world_heritage/heritage/itsukushima.php|website=www.homemate-research-tour.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=MORNING ALIVE BLOG|url=http://hfm.jp/blog/morning/2008/01/post_205.html|website=hfm.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Miyajima by the Numbers - Miyajima Tourism Association|url=https://www.miyajima.or.jp/pickup/number.html|website=www.miyajima.or.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref>
== List of Stages ==
* '''Shitenno-ji Temple''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage is located at the "Turtle Pond" and is registered as a nationally designated cultural heritage site.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Stone Stage and the Toyotomi Family|url=https://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/|website=Sumiyoshi Taisha|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Shitenno-ji's Stone Stage at Turtle Pond - One of Nihon San Butai|url=https://www.tripadvisor.com.tr/LocationPhotoDirectLink-g298566-d321316-i379394922-Shitennoji-Osaka_Osaka_Prefecture_Kinki.html|website=Tripadvisor|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=tr}}</ref> * '''Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage was donated by '''Toyotomi Hideyori''' during the Keicho era and is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Osaka Scenic Information Site|url=https://osaka-keikan.com/view.php?pid=39|website=osaka-keikan.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref> * '''Itsukushima Shrine''' (Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture) - Also known as the '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''', this wooden board stage is used for '''Noh performances''' and is designated as a '''National Treasure of Japan'''.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Nihon San Butai|url=https://nippon-now.com/spots/detail/spot34.html|website=NIPPON NOW|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Japan's Three Scenic Views - The Floating Shrine of Itsukushima|url=https://rtrp.jp/articles/981/|website=RETRIP - Travel Media|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref>

[[File:Sumiyoshi jinja Otaue.jpg|thumb|360px|Sumiyoshi Taisha Otaue Ritual.]]
'''Nihon San Butai''' (日本三舞台) refers to three historic stages built for the performance of [[Bugaku]] in Japan. These stages are located at the following significant temple and shrine sites:
* The '''Stone Stage of Shitenno-ji Temple''' in [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. * The '''Stone Stage of Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' in Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture. * The '''Board Stage of Itsukushima Shrine''' (also known as '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''') in [[Hatsukaichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nihon San Butai Explained|url=https://tabi-mag.jp/stage-big3/|website=Japan Travel Magazine|date=2020-02-04|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Japan's World Heritage Sites|url=https://www.homemate-research-tour.com/useful/jp_world_heritage/heritage/itsukushima.php|website=www.homemate-research-tour.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=MORNING ALIVE BLOG|url=http://hfm.jp/blog/morning/2008/01/post_205.html|website=hfm.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Miyajima by the Numbers - Miyajima Tourism Association|url=https://www.miyajima.or.jp/pickup/number.html|website=www.miyajima.or.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref>
== List of Stages ==
* '''Shitenno-ji Temple''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage is located at the "Turtle Pond" and is registered as a nationally designated cultural heritage site.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Stone Stage and the Toyotomi Family|url=https://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/|website=Sumiyoshi Taisha|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Shitenno-ji's Stone Stage at Turtle Pond - One of Nihon San Butai|url=https://www.tripadvisor.com.tr/LocationPhotoDirectLink-g298566-d321316-i379394922-Shitennoji-Osaka_Osaka_Prefecture_Kinki.html|website=Tripadvisor|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=tr}}</ref> * '''Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage was donated by '''Toyotomi Hideyori''' during the Keicho era and is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Osaka Scenic Information Site|url=https://osaka-keikan.com/view.php?pid=39|website=osaka-keikan.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref> * '''Itsukushima Shrine''' (Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture) - Also known as the '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''', this wooden board stage is used for '''Noh performances''' and is designated as a '''National Treasure of Japan'''.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Nihon San Butai|url=https://nippon-now.com/spots/detail/spot34.html|website=NIPPON NOW|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Japan's Three Scenic Views - The Floating Shrine of Itsukushima|url=https://rtrp.jp/articles/981/|website=RETRIP - Travel Media|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref>

revision.text:
[[File:Sumiyoshi jinja Otaue.jpg|thumb|360px|Sumiyoshi Taisha Otaue Ritual.]]
'''Nihon San Butai''' (日本三舞台) refers to three historic stages built for the performance of [[Bugaku]] in Japan. These stages are located at the following significant temple and shrine sites:
* The '''Stone Stage of Shitenno-ji Temple''' in [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. * The '''Stone Stage of Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' in Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture. * The '''Board Stage of Itsukushima Shrine''' (also known as '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''') in [[Hatsukaichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nihon San Butai Explained|url=https://tabi-mag.jp/stage-big3/|website=Japan Travel Magazine|date=2020-02-04|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Japan's World Heritage Sites|url=https://www.homemate-research-tour.com/useful/jp_world_heritage/heritage/itsukushima.php|website=www.homemate-research-tour.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=MORNING ALIVE BLOG|url=http://hfm.jp/blog/morning/2008/01/post_205.html|website=hfm.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Miyajima by the Numbers - Miyajima Tourism Association|url=https://www.miyajima.or.jp/pickup/number.html|website=www.miyajima.or.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref>
== List of Stages ==
* '''Shitenno-ji Temple''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage is located at the "Turtle Pond" and is registered as a nationally designated cultural heritage site.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Stone Stage and the Toyotomi Family|url=https://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/|website=Sumiyoshi Taisha|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Shitenno-ji's Stone Stage at Turtle Pond - One of Nihon San Butai|url=https://www.tripadvisor.com.tr/LocationPhotoDirectLink-g298566-d321316-i379394922-Shitennoji-Osaka_Osaka_Prefecture_Kinki.html|website=Tripadvisor|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=tr}}</ref> * '''Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage was donated by '''Toyotomi Hideyori''' during the Keicho era and is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Osaka Scenic Information Site|url=https://osaka-keikan.com/view.php?pid=39|website=osaka-keikan.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref> * '''Itsukushima Shrine''' (Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture) - Also known as the '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''', this wooden board stage is used for '''Noh performances''' and is designated as a '''National Treasure of Japan'''.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Nihon San Butai|url=https://nippon-now.com/spots/detail/spot34.html|website=NIPPON NOW|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Japan's Three Scenic Views - The Floating Shrine of Itsukushima|url=https://rtrp.jp/articles/981/|website=RETRIP - Travel Media|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref>

[[File:Sumiyoshi jinja Otaue.jpg|thumb|360px|Sumiyoshi Taisha Otaue Ritual.]]
'''Nihon San Butai''' (日本三舞台) refers to three historic stages built for the performance of [[Bugaku]] in Japan. These stages are located at the following significant temple and shrine sites:
* The '''Stone Stage of Shitenno-ji Temple''' in [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. * The '''Stone Stage of Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' in Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture. * The '''Board Stage of Itsukushima Shrine''' (also known as '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''') in [[Hatsukaichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nihon San Butai Explained|url=https://tabi-mag.jp/stage-big3/|website=Japan Travel Magazine|date=2020-02-04|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Japan's World Heritage Sites|url=https://www.homemate-research-tour.com/useful/jp_world_heritage/heritage/itsukushima.php|website=www.homemate-research-tour.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=MORNING ALIVE BLOG|url=http://hfm.jp/blog/morning/2008/01/post_205.html|website=hfm.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Miyajima by the Numbers - Miyajima Tourism Association|url=https://www.miyajima.or.jp/pickup/number.html|website=www.miyajima.or.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref>
== List of Stages ==
* '''Shitenno-ji Temple''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage is located at the "Turtle Pond" and is registered as a nationally designated cultural heritage site.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Stone Stage and the Toyotomi Family|url=https://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/|website=Sumiyoshi Taisha|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Shitenno-ji's Stone Stage at Turtle Pond - One of Nihon San Butai|url=https://www.tripadvisor.com.tr/LocationPhotoDirectLink-g298566-d321316-i379394922-Shitennoji-Osaka_Osaka_Prefecture_Kinki.html|website=Tripadvisor|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=tr}}</ref> * '''Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage was donated by '''Toyotomi Hideyori''' during the Keicho era and is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Osaka Scenic Information Site|url=https://osaka-keikan.com/view.php?pid=39|website=osaka-keikan.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref> * '''Itsukushima Shrine''' (Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture) - Also known as the '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''', this wooden board stage is used for '''Noh performances''' and is designated as a '''National Treasure of Japan'''.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Nihon San Butai|url=https://nippon-now.com/spots/detail/spot34.html|website=NIPPON NOW|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Japan's Three Scenic Views - The Floating Shrine of Itsukushima|url=https://rtrp.jp/articles/981/|website=RETRIP - Travel Media|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref>

[[File:Sumiyoshi jinja Otaue.jpg|thumb|360px|Sumiyoshi Taisha Otaue Ritual.]]
'''Nihon San Butai''' (日本三舞台) refers to three historic stages built for the performance of [[Bugaku]] in Japan. These stages are located at the following significant temple and shrine sites:
* The '''Stone Stage of Shitenno-ji Temple''' in [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. * The '''Stone Stage of Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' in Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture. * The '''Board Stage of Itsukushima Shrine''' (also known as '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''') in [[Hatsukaichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nihon San Butai Explained|url=https://tabi-mag.jp/stage-big3/|website=Japan Travel Magazine|date=2020-02-04|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Japan's World Heritage Sites|url=https://www.homemate-research-tour.com/useful/jp_world_heritage/heritage/itsukushima.php|website=www.homemate-research-tour.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=MORNING ALIVE BLOG|url=http://hfm.jp/blog/morning/2008/01/post_205.html|website=hfm.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Miyajima by the Numbers - Miyajima Tourism Association|url=https://www.miyajima.or.jp/pickup/number.html|website=www.miyajima.or.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref>
== List of Stages ==
* '''Shitenno-ji Temple''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage is located at the "Turtle Pond" and is registered as a nationally designated cultural heritage site.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Stone Stage and the Toyotomi Family|url=https://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/|website=Sumiyoshi Taisha|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Shitenno-ji's Stone Stage at Turtle Pond - One of Nihon San Butai|url=https://www.tripadvisor.com.tr/LocationPhotoDirectLink-g298566-d321316-i379394922-Shitennoji-Osaka_Osaka_Prefecture_Kinki.html|website=Tripadvisor|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=tr}}</ref> * '''Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage was donated by '''Toyotomi Hideyori''' during the Keicho era and is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Osaka Scenic Information Site|url=https://osaka-keikan.com/view.php?pid=39|website=osaka-keikan.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref> * '''Itsukushima Shrine''' (Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture) - Also known as the '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''', this wooden board stage is used for '''Noh performances''' and is designated as a '''National Treasure of Japan'''.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Nihon San Butai|url=https://nippon-now.com/spots/detail/spot34.html|website=NIPPON NOW|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Japan's Three Scenic Views - The Floating Shrine of Itsukushima|url=https://rtrp.jp/articles/981/|website=RETRIP - Travel Media|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref>

'''Nihon San Butai''' (日本三舞台) refers to three historic stages built for the performance of [[Bugaku]] in Japan. These stages are located at the following significant temple and shrine sites:
* The '''Stone Stage of Shitenno-ji Temple''' in [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. * The '''Stone Stage of Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' in Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture. * The '''Board Stage of Itsukushima Shrine''' (also known as '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''') in [[Hatsukaichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nihon San Butai Explained|url=https://tabi-mag.jp/stage-big3/|website=Japan Travel Magazine|date=2020-02-04|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Japan's World Heritage Sites|url=https://www.homemate-research-tour.com/useful/jp_world_heritage/heritage/itsukushima.php|website=www.homemate-research-tour.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=MORNING ALIVE BLOG|url=http://hfm.jp/blog/morning/2008/01/post_205.html|website=hfm.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Miyajima by the Numbers - Miyajima Tourism Association|url=https://www.miyajima.or.jp/pickup/number.html|website=www.miyajima.or.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref>

[[File:Sumiyoshi jinja Otaue.jpg|thumb|360px|Sumiyoshi Taisha Otaue Ritual.]]
'''Nihon San Butai''' (日本三舞台) refers to three historic stages built for the performance of [[Bugaku]] in Japan. These stages are located at the following significant temple and shrine sites:
* The '''Stone Stage of Shitenno-ji Temple''' in [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. * The '''Stone Stage of Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' in Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture. * The '''Board Stage of Itsukushima Shrine''' (also known as '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''') in [[Hatsukaichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nihon San Butai Explained|url=https://tabi-mag.jp/stage-big3/|website=Japan Travel Magazine|date=2020-02-04|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Japan's World Heritage Sites|url=https://www.homemate-research-tour.com/useful/jp_world_heritage/heritage/itsukushima.php|website=www.homemate-research-tour.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=MORNING ALIVE BLOG|url=http://hfm.jp/blog/morning/2008/01/post_205.html|website=hfm.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Miyajima by the Numbers - Miyajima Tourism Association|url=https://www.miyajima.or.jp/pickup/number.html|website=www.miyajima.or.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref>
== List of Stages ==
* '''Shitenno-ji Temple''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage is located at the "Turtle Pond" and is registered as a nationally designated cultural heritage site.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Stone Stage and the Toyotomi Family|url=https://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/|website=Sumiyoshi Taisha|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Shitenno-ji's Stone Stage at Turtle Pond - One of Nihon San Butai|url=https://www.tripadvisor.com.tr/LocationPhotoDirectLink-g298566-d321316-i379394922-Shitennoji-Osaka_Osaka_Prefecture_Kinki.html|website=Tripadvisor|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=tr}}</ref> * '''Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage was donated by '''Toyotomi Hideyori''' during the Keicho era and is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Osaka Scenic Information Site|url=https://osaka-keikan.com/view.php?pid=39|website=osaka-keikan.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref> * '''Itsukushima Shrine''' (Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture) - Also known as the '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''', this wooden board stage is used for '''Noh performances''' and is designated as a '''National Treasure of Japan'''.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Nihon San Butai|url=https://nippon-now.com/spots/detail/spot34.html|website=NIPPON NOW|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Japan's Three Scenic Views - The Floating Shrine of Itsukushima|url=https://rtrp.jp/articles/981/|website=RETRIP - Travel Media|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref>

[[File:Sumiyoshi jinja Otaue.jpg|thumb|360px|Sumiyoshi Taisha Otaue Ritual.]]
'''Nihon San Butai''' (日本三舞台) refers to three historic stages built for the performance of [[Bugaku]] in Japan. These stages are located at the following significant temple and shrine sites:
* The '''Stone Stage of Shitenno-ji Temple''' in [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. * The '''Stone Stage of Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' in Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture. * The '''Board Stage of Itsukushima Shrine''' (also known as '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''') in [[Hatsukaichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nihon San Butai Explained|url=https://tabi-mag.jp/stage-big3/|website=Japan Travel Magazine|date=2020-02-04|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Japan's World Heritage Sites|url=https://www.homemate-research-tour.com/useful/jp_world_heritage/heritage/itsukushima.php|website=www.homemate-research-tour.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=MORNING ALIVE BLOG|url=http://hfm.jp/blog/morning/2008/01/post_205.html|website=hfm.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Miyajima by the Numbers - Miyajima Tourism Association|url=https://www.miyajima.or.jp/pickup/number.html|website=www.miyajima.or.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref>
== List of Stages ==
* '''Shitenno-ji Temple''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage is located at the "Turtle Pond" and is registered as a nationally designated cultural heritage site.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Stone Stage and the Toyotomi Family|url=https://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/|website=Sumiyoshi Taisha|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Shitenno-ji's Stone Stage at Turtle Pond - One of Nihon San Butai|url=https://www.tripadvisor.com.tr/LocationPhotoDirectLink-g298566-d321316-i379394922-Shitennoji-Osaka_Osaka_Prefecture_Kinki.html|website=Tripadvisor|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=tr}}</ref> * '''Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage was donated by '''Toyotomi Hideyori''' during the Keicho era and is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Osaka Scenic Information Site|url=https://osaka-keikan.com/view.php?pid=39|website=osaka-keikan.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref> * '''Itsukushima Shrine''' (Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture) - Also known as the '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''', this wooden board stage is used for '''Noh performances''' and is designated as a '''National Treasure of Japan'''.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Nihon San Butai|url=https://nippon-now.com/spots/detail/spot34.html|website=NIPPON NOW|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Japan's Three Scenic Views - The Floating Shrine of Itsukushima|url=https://rtrp.jp/articles/981/|website=RETRIP - Travel Media|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref>

[[File:Sumiyoshi jinja Otaue.jpg|thumb|360px|Sumiyoshi Taisha Otaue Ritual.]]
'''Nihon San Butai''' (日本三舞台) refers to three historic stages built for the performance of [[Bugaku]] in Japan. These stages are located at the following significant temple and shrine sites:
* The '''Stone Stage of Shitenno-ji Temple''' in [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. * The '''Stone Stage of Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' in Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture. * The '''Board Stage of Itsukushima Shrine''' (also known as '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''') in [[Hatsukaichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nihon San Butai Explained|url=https://tabi-mag.jp/stage-big3/|website=Japan Travel Magazine|date=2020-02-04|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Japan's World Heritage Sites|url=https://www.homemate-research-tour.com/useful/jp_world_heritage/heritage/itsukushima.php|website=www.homemate-research-tour.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=MORNING ALIVE BLOG|url=http://hfm.jp/blog/morning/2008/01/post_205.html|website=hfm.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Miyajima by the Numbers - Miyajima Tourism Association|url=https://www.miyajima.or.jp/pickup/number.html|website=www.miyajima.or.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref>
== List of Stages ==
* '''Shitenno-ji Temple''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage is located at the "Turtle Pond" and is registered as a nationally designated cultural heritage site.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Stone Stage and the Toyotomi Family|url=https://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/|website=Sumiyoshi Taisha|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Shitenno-ji's Stone Stage at Turtle Pond - One of Nihon San Butai|url=https://www.tripadvisor.com.tr/LocationPhotoDirectLink-g298566-d321316-i379394922-Shitennoji-Osaka_Osaka_Prefecture_Kinki.html|website=Tripadvisor|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=tr}}</ref> * '''Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage was donated by '''Toyotomi Hideyori''' during the Keicho era and is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Osaka Scenic Information Site|url=https://osaka-keikan.com/view.php?pid=39|website=osaka-keikan.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref> * '''Itsukushima Shrine''' (Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture) - Also known as the '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''', this wooden board stage is used for '''Noh performances''' and is designated as a '''National Treasure of Japan'''.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Nihon San Butai|url=https://nippon-now.com/spots/detail/spot34.html|website=NIPPON NOW|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Japan's Three Scenic Views - The Floating Shrine of Itsukushima|url=https://rtrp.jp/articles/981/|website=RETRIP - Travel Media|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref>

[[File:Sumiyoshi jinja Otaue.jpg|thumb|360px|Sumiyoshi Taisha Otaue Ritual.]]
'''Nihon San Butai''' (日本三舞台) refers to three historic stages built for the performance of [[Bugaku]] in Japan. These stages are located at the following significant temple and shrine sites:
* The '''Stone Stage of Shitenno-ji Temple''' in [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. * The '''Stone Stage of Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' in Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture. * The '''Board Stage of Itsukushima Shrine''' (also known as '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''') in [[Hatsukaichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nihon San Butai Explained|url=https://tabi-mag.jp/stage-big3/|website=Japan Travel Magazine|date=2020-02-04|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Japan's World Heritage Sites|url=https://www.homemate-research-tour.com/useful/jp_world_heritage/heritage/itsukushima.php|website=www.homemate-research-tour.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=MORNING ALIVE BLOG|url=http://hfm.jp/blog/morning/2008/01/post_205.html|website=hfm.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Miyajima by the Numbers - Miyajima Tourism Association|url=https://www.miyajima.or.jp/pickup/number.html|website=www.miyajima.or.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref>
== List of Stages ==
* '''Shitenno-ji Temple''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage is located at the "Turtle Pond" and is registered as a nationally designated cultural heritage site.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Stone Stage and the Toyotomi Family|url=https://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/|website=Sumiyoshi Taisha|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Shitenno-ji's Stone Stage at Turtle Pond - One of Nihon San Butai|url=https://www.tripadvisor.com.tr/LocationPhotoDirectLink-g298566-d321316-i379394922-Shitennoji-Osaka_Osaka_Prefecture_Kinki.html|website=Tripadvisor|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=tr}}</ref> * '''Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage was donated by '''Toyotomi Hideyori''' during the Keicho era and is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Osaka Scenic Information Site|url=https://osaka-keikan.com/view.php?pid=39|website=osaka-keikan.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref> * '''Itsukushima Shrine''' (Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture) - Also known as the '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''', this wooden board stage is used for '''Noh performances''' and is designated as a '''National Treasure of Japan'''.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Nihon San Butai|url=https://nippon-now.com/spots/detail/spot34.html|website=NIPPON NOW|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Japan's Three Scenic Views - The Floating Shrine of Itsukushima|url=https://rtrp.jp/articles/981/|website=RETRIP - Travel Media|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref>

[[File:Sumiyoshi jinja Otaue.jpg|thumb|360px|Sumiyoshi Taisha Otaue Ritual.]]
'''Nihon San Butai''' (日本三舞台) refers to three historic stages built for the performance of [[Bugaku]] in Japan. These stages are located at the following significant temple and shrine sites:
* The '''Stone Stage of Shitenno-ji Temple''' in [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. * The '''Stone Stage of Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' in Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture. * The '''Board Stage of Itsukushima Shrine''' (also known as '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''') in [[Hatsukaichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nihon San Butai Explained|url=https://tabi-mag.jp/stage-big3/|website=Japan Travel Magazine|date=2020-02-04|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Japan's World Heritage Sites|url=https://www.homemate-research-tour.com/useful/jp_world_heritage/heritage/itsukushima.php|website=www.homemate-research-tour.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=MORNING ALIVE BLOG|url=http://hfm.jp/blog/morning/2008/01/post_205.html|website=hfm.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Miyajima by the Numbers - Miyajima Tourism Association|url=https://www.miyajima.or.jp/pickup/number.html|website=www.miyajima.or.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref>
== List of Stages ==
* '''Shitenno-ji Temple''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage is located at the "Turtle Pond" and is registered as a nationally designated cultural heritage site.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Stone Stage and the Toyotomi Family|url=https://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/|website=Sumiyoshi Taisha|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Shitenno-ji's Stone Stage at Turtle Pond - One of Nihon San Butai|url=https://www.tripadvisor.com.tr/LocationPhotoDirectLink-g298566-d321316-i379394922-Shitennoji-Osaka_Osaka_Prefecture_Kinki.html|website=Tripadvisor|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=tr}}</ref> * '''Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage was donated by '''Toyotomi Hideyori''' during the Keicho era and is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Osaka Scenic Information Site|url=https://osaka-keikan.com/view.php?pid=39|website=osaka-keikan.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref> * '''Itsukushima Shrine''' (Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture) - Also known as the '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''', this wooden board stage is used for '''Noh performances''' and is designated as a '''National Treasure of Japan'''.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Nihon San Butai|url=https://nippon-now.com/spots/detail/spot34.html|website=NIPPON NOW|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Japan's Three Scenic Views - The Floating Shrine of Itsukushima|url=https://rtrp.jp/articles/981/|website=RETRIP - Travel Media|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref>

[[File:Sumiyoshi jinja Otaue.jpg|thumb|360px|Sumiyoshi Taisha Otaue Ritual.]]
'''Nihon San Butai''' (日本三舞台) refers to three historic stages built for the performance of [[Bugaku]] in Japan. These stages are located at the following significant temple and shrine sites:
* The '''Stone Stage of Shitenno-ji Temple''' in [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. * The '''Stone Stage of Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' in Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture. * The '''Board Stage of Itsukushima Shrine''' (also known as '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''') in [[Hatsukaichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nihon San Butai Explained|url=https://tabi-mag.jp/stage-big3/|website=Japan Travel Magazine|date=2020-02-04|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Japan's World Heritage Sites|url=https://www.homemate-research-tour.com/useful/jp_world_heritage/heritage/itsukushima.php|website=www.homemate-research-tour.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=MORNING ALIVE BLOG|url=http://hfm.jp/blog/morning/2008/01/post_205.html|website=hfm.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Miyajima by the Numbers - Miyajima Tourism Association|url=https://www.miyajima.or.jp/pickup/number.html|website=www.miyajima.or.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref>
== List of Stages ==
* '''Shitenno-ji Temple''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage is located at the "Turtle Pond" and is registered as a nationally designated cultural heritage site.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Stone Stage and the Toyotomi Family|url=https://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/|website=Sumiyoshi Taisha|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Shitenno-ji's Stone Stage at Turtle Pond - One of Nihon San Butai|url=https://www.tripadvisor.com.tr/LocationPhotoDirectLink-g298566-d321316-i379394922-Shitennoji-Osaka_Osaka_Prefecture_Kinki.html|website=Tripadvisor|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=tr}}</ref> * '''Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage was donated by '''Toyotomi Hideyori''' during the Keicho era and is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Osaka Scenic Information Site|url=https://osaka-keikan.com/view.php?pid=39|website=osaka-keikan.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref> * '''Itsukushima Shrine''' (Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture) - Also known as the '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''', this wooden board stage is used for '''Noh performances''' and is designated as a '''National Treasure of Japan'''.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Nihon San Butai|url=https://nippon-now.com/spots/detail/spot34.html|website=NIPPON NOW|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Japan's Three Scenic Views - The Floating Shrine of Itsukushima|url=https://rtrp.jp/articles/981/|website=RETRIP - Travel Media|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref>

[[File:Sumiyoshi jinja Otaue.jpg|thumb|360px|Sumiyoshi Taisha Otaue Ritual.]]
'''Nihon San Butai''' (日本三舞台) refers to three historic stages built for the performance of [[Bugaku]] in Japan. These stages are located at the following significant temple and shrine sites:
* The '''Stone Stage of Shitenno-ji Temple''' in [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. * The '''Stone Stage of Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' in Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture. * The '''Board Stage of Itsukushima Shrine''' (also known as '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''') in [[Hatsukaichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nihon San Butai Explained|url=https://tabi-mag.jp/stage-big3/|website=Japan Travel Magazine|date=2020-02-04|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Japan's World Heritage Sites|url=https://www.homemate-research-tour.com/useful/jp_world_heritage/heritage/itsukushima.php|website=www.homemate-research-tour.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=MORNING ALIVE BLOG|url=http://hfm.jp/blog/morning/2008/01/post_205.html|website=hfm.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Miyajima by the Numbers - Miyajima Tourism Association|url=https://www.miyajima.or.jp/pickup/number.html|website=www.miyajima.or.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref>
== List of Stages ==
* '''Shitenno-ji Temple''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage is located at the "Turtle Pond" and is registered as a nationally designated cultural heritage site.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Stone Stage and the Toyotomi Family|url=https://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/|website=Sumiyoshi Taisha|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Shitenno-ji's Stone Stage at Turtle Pond - One of Nihon San Butai|url=https://www.tripadvisor.com.tr/LocationPhotoDirectLink-g298566-d321316-i379394922-Shitennoji-Osaka_Osaka_Prefecture_Kinki.html|website=Tripadvisor|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=tr}}</ref> * '''Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage was donated by '''Toyotomi Hideyori''' during the Keicho era and is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Osaka Scenic Information Site|url=https://osaka-keikan.com/view.php?pid=39|website=osaka-keikan.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref> * '''Itsukushima Shrine''' (Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture) - Also known as the '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''', this wooden board stage is used for '''Noh performances''' and is designated as a '''National Treasure of Japan'''.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Nihon San Butai|url=https://nippon-now.com/spots/detail/spot34.html|website=NIPPON NOW|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Japan's Three Scenic Views - The Floating Shrine of Itsukushima|url=https://rtrp.jp/articles/981/|website=RETRIP - Travel Media|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref>

[[File:Sumiyoshi jinja Otaue.jpg|thumb|360px|Sumiyoshi Taisha Otaue Ritual.]]
'''Nihon San Butai''' (日本三舞台) refers to three historic stages built for the performance of [[Bugaku]] in Japan. These stages are located at the following significant temple and shrine sites:
* The '''Stone Stage of Shitenno-ji Temple''' in [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. * The '''Stone Stage of Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' in Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture. * The '''Board Stage of Itsukushima Shrine''' (also known as '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''') in [[Hatsukaichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nihon San Butai Explained|url=https://tabi-mag.jp/stage-big3/|website=Japan Travel Magazine|date=2020-02-04|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Japan's World Heritage Sites|url=https://www.homemate-research-tour.com/useful/jp_world_heritage/heritage/itsukushima.php|website=www.homemate-research-tour.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=MORNING ALIVE BLOG|url=http://hfm.jp/blog/morning/2008/01/post_205.html|website=hfm.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Miyajima by the Numbers - Miyajima Tourism Association|url=https://www.miyajima.or.jp/pickup/number.html|website=www.miyajima.or.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref>
== List of Stages ==
* '''Shitenno-ji Temple''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage is located at the "Turtle Pond" and is registered as a nationally designated cultural heritage site.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Stone Stage and the Toyotomi Family|url=https://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/|website=Sumiyoshi Taisha|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Shitenno-ji's Stone Stage at Turtle Pond - One of Nihon San Butai|url=https://www.tripadvisor.com.tr/LocationPhotoDirectLink-g298566-d321316-i379394922-Shitennoji-Osaka_Osaka_Prefecture_Kinki.html|website=Tripadvisor|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=tr}}</ref> * '''Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage was donated by '''Toyotomi Hideyori''' during the Keicho era and is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Osaka Scenic Information Site|url=https://osaka-keikan.com/view.php?pid=39|website=osaka-keikan.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref> * '''Itsukushima Shrine''' (Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture) - Also known as the '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''', this wooden board stage is used for '''Noh performances''' and is designated as a '''National Treasure of Japan'''.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Nihon San Butai|url=https://nippon-now.com/spots/detail/spot34.html|website=NIPPON NOW|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Japan's Three Scenic Views - The Floating Shrine of Itsukushima|url=https://rtrp.jp/articles/981/|website=RETRIP - Travel Media|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref>

[[File:Sumiyoshi jinja Otaue.jpg|thumb|360px|Sumiyoshi Taisha Otaue Ritual.]]
'''Nihon San Butai''' (日本三舞台) refers to three historic stages built for the performance of [[Bugaku]] in Japan. These stages are located at the following significant temple and shrine sites:
* The '''Stone Stage of Shitenno-ji Temple''' in [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. * The '''Stone Stage of Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' in Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture. * The '''Board Stage of Itsukushima Shrine''' (also known as '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''') in [[Hatsukaichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nihon San Butai Explained|url=https://tabi-mag.jp/stage-big3/|website=Japan Travel Magazine|date=2020-02-04|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Japan's World Heritage Sites|url=https://www.homemate-research-tour.com/useful/jp_world_heritage/heritage/itsukushima.php|website=www.homemate-research-tour.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=MORNING ALIVE BLOG|url=http://hfm.jp/blog/morning/2008/01/post_205.html|website=hfm.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Miyajima by the Numbers - Miyajima Tourism Association|url=https://www.miyajima.or.jp/pickup/number.html|website=www.miyajima.or.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref>
== List of Stages ==
* '''Shitenno-ji Temple''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage is located at the "Turtle Pond" and is registered as a nationally designated cultural heritage site.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Stone Stage and the Toyotomi Family|url=https://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/|website=Sumiyoshi Taisha|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Shitenno-ji's Stone Stage at Turtle Pond - One of Nihon San Butai|url=https://www.tripadvisor.com.tr/LocationPhotoDirectLink-g298566-d321316-i379394922-Shitennoji-Osaka_Osaka_Prefecture_Kinki.html|website=Tripadvisor|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=tr}}</ref> * '''Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage was donated by '''Toyotomi Hideyori''' during the Keicho era and is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Osaka Scenic Information Site|url=https://osaka-keikan.com/view.php?pid=39|website=osaka-keikan.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref> * '''Itsukushima Shrine''' (Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture) - Also known as the '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''', this wooden board stage is used for '''Noh performances''' and is designated as a '''National Treasure of Japan'''.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Nihon San Butai|url=https://nippon-now.com/spots/detail/spot34.html|website=NIPPON NOW|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Japan's Three Scenic Views - The Floating Shrine of Itsukushima|url=https://rtrp.jp/articles/981/|website=RETRIP - Travel Media|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref>

[[File:Sumiyoshi jinja Otaue.jpg|thumb|360px|Sumiyoshi Taisha Otaue Ritual.]]
'''Nihon San Butai''' (日本三舞台) refers to three historic stages built for the performance of [[Bugaku]] in Japan. These stages are located at the following significant temple and shrine sites:
* The '''Stone Stage of Shitenno-ji Temple''' in [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. * The '''Stone Stage of Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' in Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture. * The '''Board Stage of Itsukushima Shrine''' (also known as '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''') in [[Hatsukaichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nihon San Butai Explained|url=https://tabi-mag.jp/stage-big3/|website=Japan Travel Magazine|date=2020-02-04|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Japan's World Heritage Sites|url=https://www.homemate-research-tour.com/useful/jp_world_heritage/heritage/itsukushima.php|website=www.homemate-research-tour.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=MORNING ALIVE BLOG|url=http://hfm.jp/blog/morning/2008/01/post_205.html|website=hfm.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Miyajima by the Numbers - Miyajima Tourism Association|url=https://www.miyajima.or.jp/pickup/number.html|website=www.miyajima.or.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref>
== List of Stages ==
* '''Shitenno-ji Temple''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage is located at the "Turtle Pond" and is registered as a nationally designated cultural heritage site.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Stone Stage and the Toyotomi Family|url=https://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/|website=Sumiyoshi Taisha|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Shitenno-ji's Stone Stage at Turtle Pond - One of Nihon San Butai|url=https://www.tripadvisor.com.tr/LocationPhotoDirectLink-g298566-d321316-i379394922-Shitennoji-Osaka_Osaka_Prefecture_Kinki.html|website=Tripadvisor|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=tr}}</ref> * '''Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage was donated by '''Toyotomi Hideyori''' during the Keicho era and is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Osaka Scenic Information Site|url=https://osaka-keikan.com/view.php?pid=39|website=osaka-keikan.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref> * '''Itsukushima Shrine''' (Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture) - Also known as the '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''', this wooden board stage is used for '''Noh performances''' and is designated as a '''National Treasure of Japan'''.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Nihon San Butai|url=https://nippon-now.com/spots/detail/spot34.html|website=NIPPON NOW|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Japan's Three Scenic Views - The Floating Shrine of Itsukushima|url=https://rtrp.jp/articles/981/|website=RETRIP - Travel Media|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref>

[[File:Sumiyoshi jinja Otaue.jpg|thumb|360px|Sumiyoshi Taisha Otaue Ritual.]]
'''Nihon San Butai''' (日本三舞台) refers to three historic stages built for the performance of [[Bugaku]] in Japan. These stages are located at the following significant temple and shrine sites:
* The '''Stone Stage of Shitenno-ji Temple''' in [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. * The '''Stone Stage of Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' in Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture. * The '''Board Stage of Itsukushima Shrine''' (also known as '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''') in [[Hatsukaichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nihon San Butai Explained|url=https://tabi-mag.jp/stage-big3/|website=Japan Travel Magazine|date=2020-02-04|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Japan's World Heritage Sites|url=https://www.homemate-research-tour.com/useful/jp_world_heritage/heritage/itsukushima.php|website=www.homemate-research-tour.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=MORNING ALIVE BLOG|url=http://hfm.jp/blog/morning/2008/01/post_205.html|website=hfm.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Miyajima by the Numbers - Miyajima Tourism Association|url=https://www.miyajima.or.jp/pickup/number.html|website=www.miyajima.or.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref>
== List of Stages ==
* '''Shitenno-ji Temple''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage is located at the "Turtle Pond" and is registered as a nationally designated cultural heritage site.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Stone Stage and the Toyotomi Family|url=https://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/|website=Sumiyoshi Taisha|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Shitenno-ji's Stone Stage at Turtle Pond - One of Nihon San Butai|url=https://www.tripadvisor.com.tr/LocationPhotoDirectLink-g298566-d321316-i379394922-Shitennoji-Osaka_Osaka_Prefecture_Kinki.html|website=Tripadvisor|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=tr}}</ref> * '''Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage was donated by '''Toyotomi Hideyori''' during the Keicho era and is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Osaka Scenic Information Site|url=https://osaka-keikan.com/view.php?pid=39|website=osaka-keikan.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref> * '''Itsukushima Shrine''' (Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture) - Also known as the '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''', this wooden board stage is used for '''Noh performances''' and is designated as a '''National Treasure of Japan'''.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Nihon San Butai|url=https://nippon-now.com/spots/detail/spot34.html|website=NIPPON NOW|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Japan's Three Scenic Views - The Floating Shrine of Itsukushima|url=https://rtrp.jp/articles/981/|website=RETRIP - Travel Media|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref>

'''Nihon San Butai''' (日本三舞台) refers to three historic stages built for the performance of [[Bugaku]] in Japan. These stages are located at the following significant temple and shrine sites:
* The '''Stone Stage of Shitenno-ji Temple''' in [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. * The '''Stone Stage of Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' in Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture. * The '''Board Stage of Itsukushima Shrine''' (also known as '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''') in [[Hatsukaichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nihon San Butai Explained|url=https://tabi-mag.jp/stage-big3/|website=Japan Travel Magazine|date=2020-02-04|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Japan's World Heritage Sites|url=https://www.homemate-research-tour.com/useful/jp_world_heritage/heritage/itsukushima.php|website=www.homemate-research-tour.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=MORNING ALIVE BLOG|url=http://hfm.jp/blog/morning/2008/01/post_205.html|website=hfm.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Miyajima by the Numbers - Miyajima Tourism Association|url=https://www.miyajima.or.jp/pickup/number.html|website=www.miyajima.or.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref>
== List of Stages ==
* '''Shitenno-ji Temple''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage is located at the "Turtle Pond" and is registered as a nationally designated cultural heritage site.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Stone Stage and the Toyotomi Family|url=https://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/|website=Sumiyoshi Taisha|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Shitenno-ji's Stone Stage at Turtle Pond - One of Nihon San Butai|url=https://www.tripadvisor.com.tr/LocationPhotoDirectLink-g298566-d321316-i379394922-Shitennoji-Osaka_Osaka_Prefecture_Kinki.html|website=Tripadvisor|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=tr}}</ref> * '''Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage was donated by '''Toyotomi Hideyori''' during the Keicho era and is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Osaka Scenic Information Site|url=https://osaka-keikan.com/view.php?pid=39|website=osaka-keikan.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref> * '''Itsukushima Shrine''' (Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture) - Also known as the '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''', this wooden board stage is used for '''Noh performances''' and is designated as a '''National Treasure of Japan'''.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Nihon San Butai|url=https://nippon-now.com/spots/detail/spot34.html|website=NIPPON NOW|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Japan's Three Scenic Views - The Floating Shrine of Itsukushima|url=https://rtrp.jp/articles/981/|website=RETRIP - Travel Media|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref>

[[File:Sumiyoshi jinja Otaue.jpg|thumb|360px|Sumiyoshi Taisha Otaue Ritual.]]
'''Nihon San Butai''' (日本三舞台) refers to three historic stages built for the performance of [[Bugaku]] in Japan. These stages are located at the following significant temple and shrine sites:
* The '''Stone Stage of Shitenno-ji Temple''' in [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. * The '''Stone Stage of Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' in Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture. * The '''Board Stage of Itsukushima Shrine''' (also known as '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''') in [[Hatsukaichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nihon San Butai Explained|url=https://tabi-mag.jp/stage-big3/|website=Japan Travel Magazine|date=2020-02-04|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Japan's World Heritage Sites|url=https://www.homemate-research-tour.com/useful/jp_world_heritage/heritage/itsukushima.php|website=www.homemate-research-tour.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=MORNING ALIVE BLOG|url=http://hfm.jp/blog/morning/2008/01/post_205.html|website=hfm.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Miyajima by the Numbers - Miyajima Tourism Association|url=https://www.miyajima.or.jp/pickup/number.html|website=www.miyajima.or.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref>
== List of Stages ==
* '''Shitenno-ji Temple''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage is located at the "Turtle Pond" and is registered as a nationally designated cultural heritage site.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Stone Stage and the Toyotomi Family|url=https://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/|website=Sumiyoshi Taisha|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Shitenno-ji's Stone Stage at Turtle Pond - One of Nihon San Butai|url=https://www.tripadvisor.com.tr/LocationPhotoDirectLink-g298566-d321316-i379394922-Shitennoji-Osaka_Osaka_Prefecture_Kinki.html|website=Tripadvisor|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=tr}}</ref> * '''Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage was donated by '''Toyotomi Hideyori''' during the Keicho era and is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Osaka Scenic Information Site|url=https://osaka-keikan.com/view.php?pid=39|website=osaka-keikan.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref> * '''Itsukushima Shrine''' (Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture) - Also known as the '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''', this wooden board stage is used for '''Noh performances''' and is designated as a '''National Treasure of Japan'''.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Nihon San Butai|url=https://nippon-now.com/spots/detail/spot34.html|website=NIPPON NOW|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Japan's Three Scenic Views - The Floating Shrine of Itsukushima|url=https://rtrp.jp/articles/981/|website=RETRIP - Travel Media|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref>

[[File:Sumiyoshi jinja Otaue.jpg|thumb|360px|Sumiyoshi Taisha Otaue Ritual.]]
'''Nihon San Butai''' (日本三舞台) refers to three historic stages built for the performance of [[Bugaku]] in Japan. These stages are located at the following significant temple and shrine sites:
* The '''Stone Stage of Shitenno-ji Temple''' in [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. * The '''Stone Stage of Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' in Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture. * The '''Board Stage of Itsukushima Shrine''' (also known as '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''') in [[Hatsukaichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nihon San Butai Explained|url=https://tabi-mag.jp/stage-big3/|website=Japan Travel Magazine|date=2020-02-04|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Japan's World Heritage Sites|url=https://www.homemate-research-tour.com/useful/jp_world_heritage/heritage/itsukushima.php|website=www.homemate-research-tour.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=MORNING ALIVE BLOG|url=http://hfm.jp/blog/morning/2008/01/post_205.html|website=hfm.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Miyajima by the Numbers - Miyajima Tourism Association|url=https://www.miyajima.or.jp/pickup/number.html|website=www.miyajima.or.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref>
== List of Stages ==
* '''Shitenno-ji Temple''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage is located at the "Turtle Pond" and is registered as a nationally designated cultural heritage site.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Stone Stage and the Toyotomi Family|url=https://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/|website=Sumiyoshi Taisha|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Shitenno-ji's Stone Stage at Turtle Pond - One of Nihon San Butai|url=https://www.tripadvisor.com.tr/LocationPhotoDirectLink-g298566-d321316-i379394922-Shitennoji-Osaka_Osaka_Prefecture_Kinki.html|website=Tripadvisor|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=tr}}</ref> * '''Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage was donated by '''Toyotomi Hideyori''' during the Keicho era and is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Osaka Scenic Information Site|url=https://osaka-keikan.com/view.php?pid=39|website=osaka-keikan.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref> * '''Itsukushima Shrine''' (Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture) - Also known as the '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''', this wooden board stage is used for '''Noh performances''' and is designated as a '''National Treasure of Japan'''.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Nihon San Butai|url=https://nippon-now.com/spots/detail/spot34.html|website=NIPPON NOW|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Japan's Three Scenic Views - The Floating Shrine of Itsukushima|url=https://rtrp.jp/articles/981/|website=RETRIP - Travel Media|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref>
