[[File:Sumiyoshi jinja Otaue.jpg|thumb|360px|Sumiyoshi Taisha Otaue Ritual.]]
'''Nihon San Butai''' (日本三舞台) refers to three historic stages built for the performance of [[Bugaku]] in Japan. These stages are located at the following significant temple and shrine sites:
* The '''Stone Stage of Shitenno-ji Temple''' in [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. * The '''Stone Stage of Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' in Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture. * The '''Board Stage of Itsukushima Shrine''' (also known as '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''') in [[Hatsukaichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nihon San Butai Explained|url=https://tabi-mag.jp/stage-big3/|website=Japan Travel Magazine|date=2020-02-04|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Japan's World Heritage Sites|url=https://www.homemate-research-tour.com/useful/jp_world_heritage/heritage/itsukushima.php|website=www.homemate-research-tour.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=MORNING ALIVE BLOG|url=http://hfm.jp/blog/morning/2008/01/post_205.html|website=hfm.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Miyajima by the Numbers - Miyajima Tourism Association|url=https://www.miyajima.or.jp/pickup/number.html|website=www.miyajima.or.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref>
== List of Stages ==
* '''Shitenno-ji Temple''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage is located at the "Turtle Pond" and is registered as a nationally designated cultural heritage site.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Stone Stage and the Toyotomi Family|url=https://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/|website=Sumiyoshi Taisha|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Shitenno-ji's Stone Stage at Turtle Pond - One of Nihon San Butai|url=https://www.tripadvisor.com.tr/LocationPhotoDirectLink-g298566-d321316-i379394922-Shitennoji-Osaka_Osaka_Prefecture_Kinki.html|website=Tripadvisor|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=tr}}</ref> * '''Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage was donated by '''Toyotomi Hideyori''' during the Keicho era and is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Osaka Scenic Information Site|url=https://osaka-keikan.com/view.php?pid=39|website=osaka-keikan.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref> * '''Itsukushima Shrine''' (Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture) - Also known as the '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''', this wooden board stage is used for '''Noh performances''' and is designated as a '''National Treasure of Japan'''.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Nihon San Butai|url=https://nippon-now.com/spots/detail/spot34.html|website=NIPPON NOW|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Japan's Three Scenic Views - The Floating Shrine of Itsukushima|url=https://rtrp.jp/articles/981/|website=RETRIP - Travel Media|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Theatre of Japan|lt=Traditional performing arts of Japan|en|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [https://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/events/special.html#anchor01 Otaue (Official Sumiyoshi Taisha website)] Accessed September 22, 2009. * {{Cultural Heritage Online|302|103|住吉の御田植]]

9 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:日本三舞台 up to rev 99177603
revision.text:
'''日本三舞台'''（にほんさんぶたい）は、[[舞楽]]を奉納するための舞台が備えられている、[[四天王寺]]（[[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[天王寺区]]）の石舞台、[[住吉大社]]（大阪府大阪市[[住吉区]]）の石舞台、[[厳島神社]]（別名: 平舞台、高舞台）（[[広島県]][[廿日市市]]）の板舞台からなる3つの歴史ある社寺に備えられた舞台のことをいう<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=日本三舞台とは!?|url=https://tabi-mag.jp/stage-big3/|website=ニッポン旅マガジン|date=2020-02-04|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=【旅探（たびたん）】厳島神社｜日本の世界遺産|url=https://www.homemate-research-tour.com/useful/jp_world_heritage/heritage/itsukushima.php|website=www.homemate-research-tour.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=MORNING ALIVE BLOG|url=http://hfm.jp/blog/morning/2008/01/post_205.html|website=hfm.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=数で知る宮島｜一般社団法人宮島観光協会|url=https://www.miyajima.or.jp/pickup/number.html|website=www.miyajima.or.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref>。
== 一覧 ==
* [[四天王寺]]（[[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[天王寺区]]）の石舞台 - 亀の池のところに位置し、国の重要指定文化遺産に登録されている<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=石舞台と豊臣家 {{!}} コラム|url=https://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/|website=住吉大社|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=四天王寺の石舞台。亀の池の所にあります。 住吉大社の石舞台、厳島神社の平舞台と並んで日本三舞台だそうです。 - Shitennoji, Osaka Resmi - Tripadvisor|url=https://www.tripadvisor.com.tr/LocationPhotoDirectLink-g298566-d321316-i379394922-Shitennoji-Osaka_Osaka_Prefecture_Kinki.html|website=www.tripadvisor.com.tr|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=tr}}</ref>。 * [[住吉大社]]（大阪府大阪市[[住吉区]]）の石舞台 - 慶長年間に豊臣秀頼によって奉納された地で、日本の重要文化財に指定されている<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=大阪景観情報サイトおおさか景観.com｜大阪ええ眺めセレクション|url=https://osaka-keikan.com/view.php?pid=39|website=osaka-keikan.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref> * [[厳島神社]]（別名: 平舞台、高舞台）（[[広島県]][[廿日市市]]）の板舞台 - 能の舞台としても使用されており、国宝に登録されている<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=厳島神社｜日本三舞台｜日本各地の情報をお届け|url=https://nippon-now.com/spots/detail/spot34.html|website=日本の魅力を再発見！NIPPON NOW|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=【日本三景】国内外問わず人気。海の上にたたずむ安芸の宮島「厳島神社」 {{!}} RETRIP[リトリップ]|url=https://rtrp.jp/articles/981/|website=RETRIP[リトリップ] - 旅行・おでかけメディア|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}{{Theat-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:にほんさんふたい}} [[Category:日本の劇場|*]] [[Category:日本の名数3|ふたい]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[日本三舞台]] to [[Three stages of Japan]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

[[File:Sumiyoshi jinja Otaue.jpg|thumb|360px|Sumiyoshi Taisha Otaue Ritual.]]
'''Nihon San Butai''' (日本三舞台) refers to three historic stages built for the performance of [[Bugaku]] in Japan. These stages are located at the following significant temple and shrine sites:
* The '''Stone Stage of Shitenno-ji Temple''' in [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. * The '''Stone Stage of Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' in Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture. * The '''Board Stage of Itsukushima Shrine''' (also known as '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''') in [[Hatsukaichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nihon San Butai Explained|url=https://tabi-mag.jp/stage-big3/|website=Japan Travel Magazine|date=2020-02-04|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Japan's World Heritage Sites|url=https://www.homemate-research-tour.com/useful/jp_world_heritage/heritage/itsukushima.php|website=www.homemate-research-tour.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=MORNING ALIVE BLOG|url=http://hfm.jp/blog/morning/2008/01/post_205.html|website=hfm.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Miyajima by the Numbers - Miyajima Tourism Association|url=https://www.miyajima.or.jp/pickup/number.html|website=www.miyajima.or.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref>
== List of Stages ==
* '''Shitenno-ji Temple''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage is located at the "Turtle Pond" and is registered as a nationally designated cultural heritage site.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Stone Stage and the Toyotomi Family|url=https://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/|website=Sumiyoshi Taisha|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Shitenno-ji's Stone Stage at Turtle Pond - One of Nihon San Butai|url=https://www.tripadvisor.com.tr/LocationPhotoDirectLink-g298566-d321316-i379394922-Shitennoji-Osaka_Osaka_Prefecture_Kinki.html|website=Tripadvisor|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=tr}}</ref> * '''Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage was donated by '''Toyotomi Hideyori''' during the Keicho era and is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Osaka Scenic Information Site|url=https://osaka-keikan.com/view.php?pid=39|website=osaka-keikan.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref> * '''Itsukushima Shrine''' (Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture) - Also known as the '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''', this wooden board stage is used for '''Noh performances''' and is designated as a '''National Treasure of Japan'''.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Nihon San Butai|url=https://nippon-now.com/spots/detail/spot34.html|website=NIPPON NOW|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Japan's Three Scenic Views - The Floating Shrine of Itsukushima|url=https://rtrp.jp/articles/981/|website=RETRIP - Travel Media|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Theatre of Japan|lt=Traditional performing arts of Japan|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== External Links == * [https://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/events/special.html#anchor01 Otaue (Official Sumiyoshi Taisha website)] Accessed September 22, 2009. * {{Cultural Heritage Online|302|103|住吉の御田植]]

'''Nihon San Butai''' (日本三舞台) refers to three historic stages built for the performance of [[Bugaku]] in Japan. These stages are located at the following significant temple and shrine sites:
* The '''Stone Stage of Shitenno-ji Temple''' in [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. * The '''Stone Stage of Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' in Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture. * The '''Board Stage of Itsukushima Shrine''' (also known as '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''') in [[Hatsukaichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nihon San Butai Explained|url=https://tabi-mag.jp/stage-big3/|website=Japan Travel Magazine|date=2020-02-04|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Japan's World Heritage Sites|url=https://www.homemate-research-tour.com/useful/jp_world_heritage/heritage/itsukushima.php|website=www.homemate-research-tour.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=MORNING ALIVE BLOG|url=http://hfm.jp/blog/morning/2008/01/post_205.html|website=hfm.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Miyajima by the Numbers - Miyajima Tourism Association|url=https://www.miyajima.or.jp/pickup/number.html|website=www.miyajima.or.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref>

'''Nihon San Butai''' (日本三舞台) refers to three historic stages built for the performance of [[:en:Bugaku|Bugaku]] in Japan. These stages are located at the following significant temple and shrine sites:
* The '''Stone Stage of Shitenno-ji Temple''' in [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. * The '''Stone Stage of Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' in Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture. * The '''Board Stage of Itsukushima Shrine''' (also known as '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''') in [[Hatsukaichi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nihon San Butai Explained|url=https://tabi-mag.jp/stage-big3/|website=Japan Travel Magazine|date=2020-02-04|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Japan's World Heritage Sites|url=https://www.homemate-research-tour.com/useful/jp_world_heritage/heritage/itsukushima.php|website=www.homemate-research-tour.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=MORNING ALIVE BLOG|url=http://hfm.jp/blog/morning/2008/01/post_205.html|website=hfm.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Miyajima by the Numbers - Miyajima Tourism Association|url=https://www.miyajima.or.jp/pickup/number.html|website=www.miyajima.or.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref>
== List of Stages ==
* '''Shitenno-ji Temple''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage is located at the "Turtle Pond" and is registered as a nationally designated cultural heritage site.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Stone Stage and the Toyotomi Family|url=https://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/|website=Sumiyoshi Taisha|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Shitenno-ji's Stone Stage at Turtle Pond - One of Nihon San Butai|url=https://www.tripadvisor.com.tr/LocationPhotoDirectLink-g298566-d321316-i379394922-Shitennoji-Osaka_Osaka_Prefecture_Kinki.html|website=Tripadvisor|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=tr}}</ref> * '''Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage was donated by '''Toyotomi Hideyori''' during the Keicho era and is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Osaka Scenic Information Site|url=https://osaka-keikan.com/view.php?pid=39|website=osaka-keikan.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref> * '''Itsukushima Shrine''' (Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture) - Also known as the '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''', this wooden board stage is used for '''Noh performances''' and is designated as a '''National Treasure of Japan'''.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Nihon San Butai|url=https://nippon-now.com/spots/detail/spot34.html|website=NIPPON NOW|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Japan's Three Scenic Views - The Floating Shrine of Itsukushima|url=https://rtrp.jp/articles/981/|website=RETRIP - Travel Media|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref>

revision.text:
[[File:Sumiyoshi jinja Otaue.jpg|thumb|360px|Sumiyoshi Taisha Otaue Ritual.]]
'''Nihon San Butai''' (日本三舞台) refers to three historic stages built for the performance of [[:en:Bugaku|Bugaku]] in Japan. These stages are located at the following significant temple and shrine sites:
* The '''Stone Stage of Shitenno-ji Temple''' in [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. * The '''Stone Stage of Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' in Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture. * The '''Board Stage of Itsukushima Shrine''' (also known as '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''') in [[Hatsukaichi City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nihon San Butai Explained|url=https://tabi-mag.jp/stage-big3/|website=Japan Travel Magazine|date=2020-02-04|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Japan's World Heritage Sites|url=https://www.homemate-research-tour.com/useful/jp_world_heritage/heritage/itsukushima.php|website=www.homemate-research-tour.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=MORNING ALIVE BLOG|url=http://hfm.jp/blog/morning/2008/01/post_205.html|website=hfm.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Miyajima by the Numbers - Miyajima Tourism Association|url=https://www.miyajima.or.jp/pickup/number.html|website=www.miyajima.or.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref>
== List of Stages ==
* '''Shitenno-ji Temple''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage is located at the "Turtle Pond" and is registered as a nationally designated cultural heritage site.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Stone Stage and the Toyotomi Family|url=https://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/|website=Sumiyoshi Taisha|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Shitenno-ji's Stone Stage at Turtle Pond - One of Nihon San Butai|url=https://www.tripadvisor.com.tr/LocationPhotoDirectLink-g298566-d321316-i379394922-Shitennoji-Osaka_Osaka_Prefecture_Kinki.html|website=Tripadvisor|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=tr}}</ref> * '''Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage was donated by '''Toyotomi Hideyori''' during the Keicho era and is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Osaka Scenic Information Site|url=https://osaka-keikan.com/view.php?pid=39|website=osaka-keikan.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref> * '''Itsukushima Shrine''' (Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture) - Also known as the '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''', this wooden board stage is used for '''Noh performances''' and is designated as a '''National Treasure of Japan'''.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Nihon San Butai|url=https://nippon-now.com/spots/detail/spot34.html|website=NIPPON NOW|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Japan's Three Scenic Views - The Floating Shrine of Itsukushima|url=https://rtrp.jp/articles/981/|website=RETRIP - Travel Media|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Theatre of Japan|lt=Traditional performing arts of Japan|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theatre of Japan|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/events/special.html#anchor01 Otaue (Official Sumiyoshi Taisha website)] Accessed September 22, 2009. * {{Cultural Heritage Online|302|103|住吉の御田植]]

* The '''Stone Stage of Shitenno-ji Temple''' in [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. * The '''Stone Stage of Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' in Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture. * The '''Board Stage of Itsukushima Shrine''' (also known as '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''') in [[Hatsukaichi City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nihon San Butai Explained|url=https://tabi-mag.jp/stage-big3/|website=Japan Travel Magazine|date=2020-02-04|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Japan's World Heritage Sites|url=https://www.homemate-research-tour.com/useful/jp_world_heritage/heritage/itsukushima.php|website=www.homemate-research-tour.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=MORNING ALIVE BLOG|url=http://hfm.jp/blog/morning/2008/01/post_205.html|website=hfm.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Miyajima by the Numbers - Miyajima Tourism Association|url=https://www.miyajima.or.jp/pickup/number.html|website=www.miyajima.or.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref>

'''Nihon San Butai''' (日本三舞台) refers to three historic stages built for the performance of [[:en:Bugaku|Bugaku]] in Japan. These stages are located at the following significant temple and shrine sites:
* The '''Stone Stage of Shitenno-ji Temple''' in [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. * The '''Stone Stage of Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' in Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture. * The '''Board Stage of Itsukushima Shrine''' (also known as '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''') in [[Hatsukaichi City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nihon San Butai Explained|url=https://tabi-mag.jp/stage-big3/|website=Japan Travel Magazine|date=2020-02-04|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Japan's World Heritage Sites|url=https://www.homemate-research-tour.com/useful/jp_world_heritage/heritage/itsukushima.php|website=www.homemate-research-tour.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=MORNING ALIVE BLOG|url=http://hfm.jp/blog/morning/2008/01/post_205.html|website=hfm.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Miyajima by the Numbers - Miyajima Tourism Association|url=https://www.miyajima.or.jp/pickup/number.html|website=www.miyajima.or.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref>

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11507123]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Sumiyoshi jinja Otaue.jpg|thumb|360px|Sumiyoshi Taisha Otaue Ritual.]]
'''Nihon San Butai''' (日本三舞台) refers to three historic stages built for the performance of [[:en:Bugaku|Bugaku]] in Japan. These stages are located at the following significant temple and shrine sites:
* The '''Stone Stage of Shitenno-ji Temple''' in [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. * The '''Stone Stage of Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' in Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture. * The '''Board Stage of Itsukushima Shrine''' (also known as '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''') in [[Hatsukaichi City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nihon San Butai Explained|url=https://tabi-mag.jp/stage-big3/|website=Japan Travel Magazine|date=2020-02-04|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Japan's World Heritage Sites|url=https://www.homemate-research-tour.com/useful/jp_world_heritage/heritage/itsukushima.php|website=www.homemate-research-tour.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=MORNING ALIVE BLOG|url=http://hfm.jp/blog/morning/2008/01/post_205.html|website=hfm.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Miyajima by the Numbers - Miyajima Tourism Association|url=https://www.miyajima.or.jp/pickup/number.html|website=www.miyajima.or.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref>
== List of Stages ==
* '''Shitenno-ji Temple''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage is located at the "Turtle Pond" and is registered as a nationally designated cultural heritage site.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Stone Stage and the Toyotomi Family|url=https://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/|website=Sumiyoshi Taisha|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Shitenno-ji's Stone Stage at Turtle Pond - One of Nihon San Butai|url=https://www.tripadvisor.com.tr/LocationPhotoDirectLink-g298566-d321316-i379394922-Shitennoji-Osaka_Osaka_Prefecture_Kinki.html|website=Tripadvisor|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=tr}}</ref> * '''Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage was donated by '''Toyotomi Hideyori''' during the Keicho era and is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Osaka Scenic Information Site|url=https://osaka-keikan.com/view.php?pid=39|website=osaka-keikan.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref> * '''Itsukushima Shrine''' (Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture) - Also known as the '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''', this wooden board stage is used for '''Noh performances''' and is designated as a '''National Treasure of Japan'''.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Nihon San Butai|url=https://nippon-now.com/spots/detail/spot34.html|website=NIPPON NOW|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Japan's Three Scenic Views - The Floating Shrine of Itsukushima|url=https://rtrp.jp/articles/981/|website=RETRIP - Travel Media|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref>

== External Links == * [https://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/events/special.html#anchor01 Otaue (Official Sumiyoshi Taisha website)] Accessed September 22, 2009. * {{Cultural Heritage Online|302|103|住吉の御田植]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:日本三舞台]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11507123]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Sumiyoshi jinja Otaue.jpg|thumb|360px|Sumiyoshi Taisha Otaue Ritual.]]
'''Nihon San Butai''' (日本三舞台) refers to three historic stages built for the performance of [[:en:Bugaku|Bugaku]] in Japan. These stages are located at the following significant temple and shrine sites:
* The '''Stone Stage of Shitenno-ji Temple''' in [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. * The '''Stone Stage of Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' in Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture. * The '''Board Stage of Itsukushima Shrine''' (also known as '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''') in [[Hatsukaichi City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nihon San Butai Explained|url=https://tabi-mag.jp/stage-big3/|website=Japan Travel Magazine|date=2020-02-04|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Japan's World Heritage Sites|url=https://www.homemate-research-tour.com/useful/jp_world_heritage/heritage/itsukushima.php|website=www.homemate-research-tour.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=MORNING ALIVE BLOG|url=http://hfm.jp/blog/morning/2008/01/post_205.html|website=hfm.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Miyajima by the Numbers - Miyajima Tourism Association|url=https://www.miyajima.or.jp/pickup/number.html|website=www.miyajima.or.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref>
== List of Stages ==
* '''Shitenno-ji Temple''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage is located at the "Turtle Pond" and is registered as a nationally designated cultural heritage site.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Stone Stage and the Toyotomi Family|url=https://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/|website=Sumiyoshi Taisha|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Shitenno-ji's Stone Stage at Turtle Pond - One of Nihon San Butai|url=https://www.tripadvisor.com.tr/LocationPhotoDirectLink-g298566-d321316-i379394922-Shitennoji-Osaka_Osaka_Prefecture_Kinki.html|website=Tripadvisor|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=tr}}</ref> * '''Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage was donated by '''Toyotomi Hideyori''' during the Keicho era and is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Osaka Scenic Information Site|url=https://osaka-keikan.com/view.php?pid=39|website=osaka-keikan.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref> * '''Itsukushima Shrine''' (Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture) - Also known as the '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''', this wooden board stage is used for '''Noh performances''' and is designated as a '''National Treasure of Japan'''.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Nihon San Butai|url=https://nippon-now.com/spots/detail/spot34.html|website=NIPPON NOW|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Japan's Three Scenic Views - The Floating Shrine of Itsukushima|url=https://rtrp.jp/articles/981/|website=RETRIP - Travel Media|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref>

'''Nihon San Butai''' (日本三舞台) refers to three historic stages built for the performance of [[:en:Bugaku|Bugaku]] in Japan. These stages are located at the following significant temple and shrine sites:
* The '''Stone Stage of Shitenno-ji Temple''' in [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. * The '''Stone Stage of Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' in Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture. * The '''Board Stage of Itsukushima Shrine''' (also known as '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''') in [[Hatsukaichi City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nihon San Butai Explained|url=https://tabi-mag.jp/stage-big3/|website=Japan Travel Magazine|date=2020-02-04|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Japan's World Heritage Sites|url=https://www.homemate-research-tour.com/useful/jp_world_heritage/heritage/itsukushima.php|website=www.homemate-research-tour.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=MORNING ALIVE BLOG|url=http://hfm.jp/blog/morning/2008/01/post_205.html|website=hfm.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Miyajima by the Numbers - Miyajima Tourism Association|url=https://www.miyajima.or.jp/pickup/number.html|website=www.miyajima.or.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref>
== List of Stages ==
* '''Shitenno-ji Temple''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage is located at the "Turtle Pond" and is registered as a nationally designated cultural heritage site.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Stone Stage and the Toyotomi Family|url=https://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/|website=Sumiyoshi Taisha|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Shitenno-ji's Stone Stage at Turtle Pond - One of Nihon San Butai|url=https://www.tripadvisor.com.tr/LocationPhotoDirectLink-g298566-d321316-i379394922-Shitennoji-Osaka_Osaka_Prefecture_Kinki.html|website=Tripadvisor|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=tr}}</ref> * '''Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage was donated by '''Toyotomi Hideyori''' during the Keicho era and is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Osaka Scenic Information Site|url=https://osaka-keikan.com/view.php?pid=39|website=osaka-keikan.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref> * '''Itsukushima Shrine''' (Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture) - Also known as the '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''', this wooden board stage is used for '''Noh performances''' and is designated as a '''National Treasure of Japan'''.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Nihon San Butai|url=https://nippon-now.com/spots/detail/spot34.html|website=NIPPON NOW|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Japan's Three Scenic Views - The Floating Shrine of Itsukushima|url=https://rtrp.jp/articles/981/|website=RETRIP - Travel Media|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Theatre of Japan|lt=Traditional performing arts of Japan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theatre of Japan|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/events/special.html#anchor01 Otaue (Official Sumiyoshi Taisha website)] Accessed September 22, 2009. * {{Cultural Heritage Online|302|103|住吉の御田植]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:日本三舞台]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11507123]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Sumiyoshi jinja Otaue.jpg|thumb|360px|Sumiyoshi Taisha Otaue Ritual.]]
'''Nihon San Butai''' (日本三舞台) refers to three historic stages built for the performance of [[:en:Bugaku|Bugaku]] in Japan. These stages are located at the following significant temple and shrine sites:
* The '''Stone Stage of Shitenno-ji Temple''' in [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. * The '''Stone Stage of Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' in Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture. * The '''Board Stage of Itsukushima Shrine''' (also known as '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''') in [[Hatsukaichi City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nihon San Butai Explained|url=https://tabi-mag.jp/stage-big3/|website=Japan Travel Magazine|date=2020-02-04|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Japan's World Heritage Sites|url=https://www.homemate-research-tour.com/useful/jp_world_heritage/heritage/itsukushima.php|website=www.homemate-research-tour.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=MORNING ALIVE BLOG|url=http://hfm.jp/blog/morning/2008/01/post_205.html|website=hfm.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Miyajima by the Numbers - Miyajima Tourism Association|url=https://www.miyajima.or.jp/pickup/number.html|website=www.miyajima.or.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref>
== List of Stages ==
* '''Shitenno-ji Temple''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage is located at the "Turtle Pond" and is registered as a nationally designated cultural heritage site.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Stone Stage and the Toyotomi Family|url=https://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/|website=Sumiyoshi Taisha|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Shitenno-ji's Stone Stage at Turtle Pond - One of Nihon San Butai|url=https://www.tripadvisor.com.tr/LocationPhotoDirectLink-g298566-d321316-i379394922-Shitennoji-Osaka_Osaka_Prefecture_Kinki.html|website=Tripadvisor|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=tr}}</ref> * '''Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage was donated by '''Toyotomi Hideyori''' during the Keicho era and is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Osaka Scenic Information Site|url=https://osaka-keikan.com/view.php?pid=39|website=osaka-keikan.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref> * '''Itsukushima Shrine''' (Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture) - Also known as the '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''', this wooden board stage is used for '''Noh performances''' and is designated as a '''National Treasure of Japan'''.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Nihon San Butai|url=https://nippon-now.com/spots/detail/spot34.html|website=NIPPON NOW|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Japan's Three Scenic Views - The Floating Shrine of Itsukushima|url=https://rtrp.jp/articles/981/|website=RETRIP - Travel Media|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref>

* '''Shitenno-ji Temple''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage is located at the "Turtle Pond" and is registered as a nationally designated cultural heritage site.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Stone Stage and the Toyotomi Family|url=https://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/|website=Sumiyoshi Taisha|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Shitenno-ji's Stone Stage at Turtle Pond - One of Nihon San Butai|url=https://www.tripadvisor.com.tr/LocationPhotoDirectLink-g298566-d321316-i379394922-Shitennoji-Osaka_Osaka_Prefecture_Kinki.html|website=Tripadvisor|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=tr}}</ref> * '''Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage was donated by '''Toyotomi Hideyori''' during the Keicho era and is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Osaka Scenic Information Site|url=https://osaka-keikan.com/view.php?pid=39|website=osaka-keikan.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref> * '''Itsukushima Shrine''' (Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture) - Also known as the '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''', this wooden board stage is used for '''Noh performances''' and is designated as a '''National Treasure of Japan'''.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Nihon San Butai|url=https://nippon-now.com/spots/detail/spot34.html|website=NIPPON NOW|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Japan's Three Scenic Views - The Floating Shrine of Itsukushima|url=https://rtrp.jp/articles/981/|website=RETRIP - Travel Media|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:日本三舞台]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:日本三舞台]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:日本三舞台]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11507123]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Sumiyoshi jinja Otaue.jpg|thumb|360px|Sumiyoshi Taisha Otaue Ritual.]]
'''Nihon San Butai''' (日本三舞台) refers to three historic stages built for the performance of {{ill|Bugaku|en|Bugaku|WD=Q282337|lt=Bugaku}} in Japan. These stages are located at the following significant temple and shrine sites:
* The '''Stone Stage of Shitenno-ji Temple''' in [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}. * The '''Stone Stage of Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' in Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture. * The '''Board Stage of Itsukushima Shrine''' (also known as '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''') in [[Hatsukaichi City]], {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|WD=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nihon San Butai Explained|url=https://tabi-mag.jp/stage-big3/|website=Japan Travel Magazine|date=2020-02-04|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Japan's World Heritage Sites|url=https://www.homemate-research-tour.com/useful/jp_world_heritage/heritage/itsukushima.php|website=www.homemate-research-tour.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=MORNING ALIVE BLOG|url=http://hfm.jp/blog/morning/2008/01/post_205.html|website=hfm.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Miyajima by the Numbers - Miyajima Tourism Association|url=https://www.miyajima.or.jp/pickup/number.html|website=www.miyajima.or.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref>
== List of Stages ==
* '''Shitenno-ji Temple''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage is located at the "Turtle Pond" and is registered as a nationally designated cultural heritage site.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Stone Stage and the Toyotomi Family|url=https://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/|website=Sumiyoshi Taisha|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Shitenno-ji's Stone Stage at Turtle Pond - One of Nihon San Butai|url=https://www.tripadvisor.com.tr/LocationPhotoDirectLink-g298566-d321316-i379394922-Shitennoji-Osaka_Osaka_Prefecture_Kinki.html|website=Tripadvisor|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=tr}}</ref> * '''Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage was donated by '''Toyotomi Hideyori''' during the Keicho era and is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Osaka Scenic Information Site|url=https://osaka-keikan.com/view.php?pid=39|website=osaka-keikan.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref> * '''Itsukushima Shrine''' (Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture) - Also known as the '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''', this wooden board stage is used for '''Noh performances''' and is designated as a '''National Treasure of Japan'''.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Nihon San Butai|url=https://nippon-now.com/spots/detail/spot34.html|website=NIPPON NOW|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Japan's Three Scenic Views - The Floating Shrine of Itsukushima|url=https://rtrp.jp/articles/981/|website=RETRIP - Travel Media|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Theatre of Japan|lt=Traditional performing arts of Japan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theatre of Japan|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/events/special.html#anchor01 Otaue (Official Sumiyoshi Taisha website)] Accessed September 22, 2009. * {{Cultural Heritage Online|302|103|住吉の御田植]]
{{Coord|34|36|40.86|N|135|29|32.83|E|region:JP-27_type:event_scale:10000|display=title]]

[[File:Sumiyoshi jinja Otaue.jpg|thumb|360px|Sumiyoshi Taisha Otaue Ritual.]]
'''Nihon San Butai''' (日本三舞台) refers to three historic stages built for the performance of {{ill|Bugaku|en|Bugaku|WD=Q282337|lt=Bugaku}} in Japan. These stages are located at the following significant temple and shrine sites:
* The '''Stone Stage of Shitenno-ji Temple''' in [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}. * The '''Stone Stage of Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' in Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture. * The '''Board Stage of Itsukushima Shrine''' (also known as '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''') in [[Hatsukaichi City]], {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|WD=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nihon San Butai Explained|url=https://tabi-mag.jp/stage-big3/|website=Japan Travel Magazine|date=2020-02-04|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Japan's World Heritage Sites|url=https://www.homemate-research-tour.com/useful/jp_world_heritage/heritage/itsukushima.php|website=www.homemate-research-tour.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=MORNING ALIVE BLOG|url=http://hfm.jp/blog/morning/2008/01/post_205.html|website=hfm.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Miyajima by the Numbers - Miyajima Tourism Association|url=https://www.miyajima.or.jp/pickup/number.html|website=www.miyajima.or.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref>
== List of Stages ==
* '''Shitenno-ji Temple''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage is located at the "Turtle Pond" and is registered as a nationally designated cultural heritage site.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Stone Stage and the Toyotomi Family|url=https://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/|website=Sumiyoshi Taisha|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Shitenno-ji's Stone Stage at Turtle Pond - One of Nihon San Butai|url=https://www.tripadvisor.com.tr/LocationPhotoDirectLink-g298566-d321316-i379394922-Shitennoji-Osaka_Osaka_Prefecture_Kinki.html|website=Tripadvisor|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=tr}}</ref> * '''Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage was donated by '''Toyotomi Hideyori''' during the Keicho era and is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Osaka Scenic Information Site|url=https://osaka-keikan.com/view.php?pid=39|website=osaka-keikan.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref> * '''Itsukushima Shrine''' (Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture) - Also known as the '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''', this wooden board stage is used for '''Noh performances''' and is designated as a '''National Treasure of Japan'''.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Nihon San Butai|url=https://nippon-now.com/spots/detail/spot34.html|website=NIPPON NOW|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Japan's Three Scenic Views - The Floating Shrine of Itsukushima|url=https://rtrp.jp/articles/981/|website=RETRIP - Travel Media|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref>

* The '''Stone Stage of Shitenno-ji Temple''' in [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. * The '''Stone Stage of Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' in Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture. * The '''Board Stage of Itsukushima Shrine''' (also known as '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''') in [[Hatsukaichi City]], {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|WD=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|1=Hiroshima Prefecture}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nihon San Butai Explained|url=https://tabi-mag.jp/stage-big3/|website=Japan Travel Magazine|date=2020-02-04|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Japan's World Heritage Sites|url=https://www.homemate-research-tour.com/useful/jp_world_heritage/heritage/itsukushima.php|website=www.homemate-research-tour.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=MORNING ALIVE BLOG|url=http://hfm.jp/blog/morning/2008/01/post_205.html|website=hfm.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Miyajima by the Numbers - Miyajima Tourism Association|url=https://www.miyajima.or.jp/pickup/number.html|website=www.miyajima.or.jp|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref>

* '''Shitenno-ji Temple''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage is located at the "Turtle Pond" and is registered as a nationally designated cultural heritage site.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Stone Stage and the Toyotomi Family|url=https://www.sumiyoshitaisha.net/|website=Sumiyoshi Taisha|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Shitenno-ji's Stone Stage at Turtle Pond - One of Nihon San Butai|url=https://www.tripadvisor.com.tr/LocationPhotoDirectLink-g298566-d321316-i379394922-Shitennoji-Osaka_Osaka_Prefecture_Kinki.html|website=Tripadvisor|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=tr}}</ref> * '''Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine''' (Osaka City, Osaka Prefecture) - The stone stage was donated by '''Toyotomi Hideyori''' during the Keicho era and is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Osaka Scenic Information Site|url=https://osaka-keikan.com/view.php?pid=39|website=osaka-keikan.com|accessdate=2021-08-13}}</ref> * '''Itsukushima Shrine''' (Hatsukaichi City, Hiroshima Prefecture) - Also known as the '''Flat Stage''' or '''High Stage''', this wooden board stage is used for '''Noh performances''' and is designated as a '''National Treasure of Japan'''.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Itsukushima Shrine - Nihon San Butai|url=https://nippon-now.com/spots/detail/spot34.html|website=NIPPON NOW|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Japan's Three Scenic Views - The Floating Shrine of Itsukushima|url=https://rtrp.jp/articles/981/|website=RETRIP - Travel Media|accessdate=2021-08-13|language=ja}}</ref>
